channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Livvy Dunne reflects on 'full-circle moment' seeing boyfriend Paul Skenes pitch against Yankees
item.description: Livvy Dunne witnessed her boyfriend, Paul Skenes, cap off his historic MLB season in Yankee Stadium, roughly 15 miles away from her hometown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Johnny Manziel wants Drake on his new podcast and promises not to ask him about Kendrick Lamar beef
item.description: Johnny Manziel told Fox News Digital that Drake is the 'dream guest' for his new podcast as he looks to make a name for himself in the media industry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Markkanen scores 35, but Jazz comeback falls short
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - It was a slow start to the season for the Utah Jazz, but they finished strong. It just wasn't enough against the Memphis Grizzlies.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: PREVIEW: Fruita vs. Central in the softball State Tournament
item.description: “It’s going to be awesome," said the always energetic Fruita head coach Adam Diaz, gleaming with excitement.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: NC State's Grayson McCall gives up football, says he can't come back from latest head injury
item.description: North Carolina State quarterback Grayson McCall has announced his retirement from football less than three weeks after a vicious hit knocked him out of a game for the second time this season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Actor, Texas fan Matthew McConaughey rips Longhorns supporters for tossing trash during loss to Georgia
item.description: Hollywood star Matthew McConaughey graduated from the University of Texas at Austin. He's a diehard Longhorns football fan and makes frequent appearances at games.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Embiid drawing criticism for sitting out 76ers' opener as part of health plan to reach the playoffs
item.description: Joel Embiid’s absence from the Philadelphia 76ers’ season opener against Milwaukee is simply part of the team’s plan to keep the two-time NBA scoring champion on track for a healthy season.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gelston, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hybricon Games enters third season
item.description: “Every year before the Hybricon Games, I’m like, ‘I’m never doing that again.’ But immediately after, I want more,” says Austen Alexander.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Tua Tagovailoa practices for the first time since suffering a concussion in Week 2
item.description: MIAMI GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Tyreek Hill couldn't hide his excitement catching the football from Tua Tagovailoa on Wednesday for the first time in over a month. “I just had a big cheese on my face,” Hill said. “It just felt so good.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Panthers QB Bryce Young will start against Broncos after Andy Dalton sprains thumb in car crash
item.description: Bryce Young will start Sunday at quarterback for the Carolina Panthers against the Denver Broncos after Andy Dalton sprained the thumb on his right throwing hand during a car crash in Charlotte.
item.dc:creator: Steve Reed, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blackhawks captain not pleased after Canucks score 4 first-period goals in 6-3 win over Chicago
item.description: Danton Heinen scored two of Vancouver's four first-period goals, and the Canucks beat the Chicago Blackhawks 6-3 on Tuesday night for their third straight win.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tatum, Celtics celebrate Banner 18 by making record-tying 29 3-pointers and crushing Knicks 132-109
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Jayson Tatum continued Boston’s Banner 18 celebration by scoring 37 points and making eight of the Celtics' NBA record-tying 29 3-pointers on Tuesday as Boston ran away with a 132-109 victory over the New York Knicks on opening night.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nestor Cortes expects to be on Yankees World Series roster, even if it's a long-term health risk
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nestor Cortes expects to be on the New York Yankees' World Series roster against the Los Angeles Dodgers after throwing two innings of batting practice and pronouncing himself recovered from an elbow injury that has sidelined him for more than a month.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bucs' Chris Godwin to have surgery, likely out for season. Fellow WR Mike Evans to miss 3 games
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Tampa Bay Buccaneers receiver Chris Godwin will undergo surgery on a dislocated left ankle, a procedure that will likely sideline him for the rest of the regular season.
item.dc:creator: FRED GOODALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tickets now on sale for San Diego's 2025 Farmers Insurance Open
item.description: This will be San Diego's 73rd year hosting a PGA Tour event and the 58th time it's being held at Torrey Pines Golf Course.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: New York Liberty win first WNBA championship, beating Minnesota 67-62 in OT
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As confetti fell and Frank Sinatra’s “New York, New York” blared through the arena, the New York Liberty celebrated the end of a long odyssey.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Russell Wilson throws for 2 TDs, runs for another TD in Pittsburgh debut as Steelers rout Jets 37-15
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Russell Wilson sat at the podium, uniform still on, in no rush to move on from a moment he believed would always come. Even as the weeks passed and his aching calf struggled to get better. Even as Justin Fields deftly guided the Pittsburgh Steelers in his place.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ticker-tape parade after NY Liberty win: Everything you need to know
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – After a massive win for the New York Liberty Sunday night, the city is ready to celebrate. On Thursday, New York City will host a ticker-tape parade for the team's first-ever WNBA championship title, Mayor Eric Adams announced. The parade will kick off at 10 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Dodgers finish off Mets with 10-5 win in NLCS
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani carried the injury-riddled Los Angeles Dodgers to baseball's best record during the regular season. Tommy Edman joined the team in late July and helped them reach the World Series. It's been that kind of year for the big-spending Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Dodgers finish off Mets with 10-5 win in NLCS and advance to face Yankees in World Series
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani carried the injury-riddled Los Angeles Dodgers to baseball's best record during the regular season. Tommy Edman joined the team in late July and helped them reach the World Series. It's been that kind of year for the big-spending Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Andy Ludwig steps down as Utes offensive coordinator
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Following another dismal offensive performance against TCU, Andy Ludwig has stepped down as Utah's offensive coordinator and quarterbacks coach. Utah head coach Kyle Whittingham released a statement Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Guardians starting Tanner Bibee in Game 5 against Yankees after short outing earlier in ALCS
item.description: Tanner Bibee is getting a second crack at the Yankees in the AL Championship Series. His first one didn't go so well. The right-hander will start for the Cleveland Guardians in Game 5 on Saturday after he was pulled from Game 2 in the second inning by manager Stephen Vogt.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New renderings show how A's ballpark would transform Las Vegas Strip
item.description: Extensive details have been revealed about the proposed $1.5 billion "multi-purpose, enclosed ballpark" on the Las Vegas Strip that could become home to the Las Vegas Athletics MLB franchise in time for the 2028 baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas Strip stadium, resort detailed in county documents; new Athletics ballpark revealed
item.description: Extensive details have been revealed about the proposed $1.5 billion "multi-purpose, enclosed ballpark" on the Las Vegas Strip that could become home to the Las Vegas Athletics MLB franchise in time for the 2028 baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, ABC 4
